normalized_title: City of Fremantle Local Planning Policy 2.8 - Fences
scope_summary: Augments the Residential Design Codes for residential fencing and sets development standards for non-residential fencing across the City of Fremantle. Replaces specified R-Codes Volume 1 deemed-to-comply clauses for primary street, secondary street and boundary fences (with a special lower height limit on Douglas Street), and prohibits electric, barbed or razor wire fencing for all development. Heritage-protected places are instead governed by LPP 3.6 where conflicts arise, and area-specific instruments prevail over this general policy.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: fences/walls within primary street setback area (residential, R-Codes Vol 1)

key_numeric_standards.topic: primary street fence visual permeability threshold
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: fences/walls within primary street setback area

key_numeric_standards.topic: Douglas Street front fence maximum height
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: primary street fences along Douglas Street, Fremantle
street fences/walls in secondary street setback area outside primary street setback area
street corner sites, secondary street setback area behind primary street setback

key_numeric_standards.unit: m above natural ground level (highest side)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: boundary fences outside primary/secondary street setback areas (residential)

key_numeric_standards.topic: attached screening material maximum additional height
key_numeric_standards.unit: mm (printed as '500m' in source - evident typo)

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: boundary fences of non-residential properties outside street setback areas

key_numeric_standards.unit: m clear of obstructions higher than 0.75m
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: all fences abutting vehicle accessways (per R-Codes cl 5.2.5, noted in policy)

cross_references.cited: Residential Design Codes (R-Codes) Volume 1, Parts B & C
Local Planning Policy 3.6 Heritage-protected Places Built Form and Land Use (LPP 3.6)
LPP 1.7: Development Exempt from Planning Approval
Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015

quality_flags: Clause 4.1.1 reads 'no more than an additional 500m high' - evidently a typo for 500mm carried from the source PDF
Two-column policy-provision tables in Part 3 are flattened in extraction (e.g. '5.2 Sightlines at vehicle / access points' split across lines) but content remains readable
